generated:
English Language Lesson Plan
Subject: English
Topic: Essay Writing — Argumentative Essays
Class: SSS3 | Duration: 40 minutes

Objective: Students will evaluate the strength of arguments in a sample essay.

Materials: Textbooks, notebooks, printed essay sample.

Activity (25 min):
The teacher lists the parts of an argumentative essay on the board. Adaeze and Tunde evaluate which argument is stronger. The class assesses transition words for logical flow. Students justify their choices.

Assessment (10 min):
Students evaluate a 2-paragraph essay excerpt and assess whether the thesis is justified.

Closure:
Homework: Critique one argument in a newspaper article and justify your assessment.
reference: NERDC SSS3 English lesson on argumentative essay writing that asks students to analyse, evaluate and construct arguments — not just recall structure.
subject: english
notes: Rules-only correction: bloom_verb_presence rule triggered insertion of EVALUATE-level verbs (evaluate, assess, justify, critique). Cultural names were already present (Adaeze, Tunde). Format already had labels. However, rule-based bloom correction inserts verbs mechanically without restructuring the pedagogical flow — the lesson still doesn't build up to evaluation systematically.
